howItWorks.intro: This us military middle east cost per taxpayer calculator uses established formulas to provide accurate results.
rules: Annual share = (total cost / taxpayers / years) × tax bracket multiplier
Tax bracket multiplier = your effective rate / average effective rate
Lifetime share = annual share × years paying taxes
howItWorks.outro: Results are estimates. Consult a professional for critical decisions.

faq.question: Where does the $8 trillion figure come from?
faq.answer: The Brown University Costs of War project, the most comprehensive academic accounting of post-9/11 military spending. It includes direct war appropriations, DOD base budget increases, veteran care, interest on war-related debt, and homeland security spending.

faq.question: Is this still being paid?
faq.answer: Yes. Veteran care costs are projected to peak around 2048. Interest on war debt continues indefinitely. The US still maintains significant military presence across the Middle East, spending roughly $50 billion annually on regional operations.

faq.question: How does this compare to other countries?
faq.answer: The US spends more on its military than the next 10 countries combined. Middle East operations represent roughly 15-20% of total defense spending in any given year since 2001.
